title: AN ACT CONVERTING THE QUIRINO HIGH SCHOOL ANNEX OF THE PINES CITY NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL LOCATED IN BARANGAY IRISAN, BAGUIO CITY, INTO A REGULAR NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL TO BE KNOWN AS THE IRISAN NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

chunks.text:
H. NO. 1709

[ REPUBLIC ACT NO. 8380, November 13, 1997 ]

AN ACT CONVERTING THE QUIRINO HIGH SCHOOL ANNEX OF THE PINES CITY NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL LOCATED IN BARANGAY IRISAN, BAGUIO CITY, INTO A REGULAR NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL TO BE KNOWN AS THE IRISAN NATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, AND APPROPRIATING FUNDS THEREFOR

Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representatives of the Philippines in Congress assembled:

SECTION 1. The Quirino High School Annex of the Pines City National High School which is located at Barangay Irisan, Baguio City, is hereby converted into a regular national high school to be known as the Irisan National High School.

SEC. 2. The amount necessary for the implementation of this Act shall be charged against the current year's appropriations of the Pines City National High School - Quirino School Annex. Thereafter, such sums as may be necessary for the operation and maintenance of the Irisan National High School shall be included in the annual General Appropriations Act.

SEC. 3. This Act shall take effect upon itsÂ approval.

Apprved,

(Sgd.) ERNESTO M. MACEDA

(Sgd.) JOSE DE VENECIA JR.

President of the Senate

Secretary of the House of Representatives

This Act which originated in the House of Representatives was finally passed by the House of Representatives and the Senate on May 24, 1989 and September 22, 1989, respectively.

(Sgd.) LORENZO E. LEYNES, JR.

(Sgd.) QUIRINO D. ABAD SANTOS, JR.

Secretary of the Senate

Secretary General House of Representatives

Approved:

FIDEL V. RAMOS

President of the Philippines

Lapsed into law on November 13, 1997 without signature of theÂ President, in accordance with Article VI, Section. 27(1), the Constitution.
